title: Rivian Q4 2024 Earnings: Gross Margin Milestone
text: Rivian achieved a significant milestone by reaching positive gross margin in Q4 2024, demonstrating substantial operational improvements. The company reported a 7% gross margin on its automotive segment with $110 million in gross profit. The automaker accomplished a $31,000 reduction in cost of goods sold per vehicle compared to the prior year quarter, reflecting effective manufacturing efficiency gains. According to CEO RJ Scaringe, We've made significant progress in reducing R1's cost of goods sold by resourcing over half of the bill of materials. Automotive revenue per unit was $86,000 excluding regulatory credits. Regulatory credit revenue was nearly $300 million for Q4. Total automotive segment revenue was $1.5 billion. Software and services gross profit reached $60 million at 28% margin. This turnaround reflects management's focus on manufacturing optimization and product mix improvements, including the introduction of higher-margin Tri-Motor offerings that boosted average selling prices.
